mnemonics.title: IPv6 Header Fields
mnemonics.content: VIP FT NHPH - Version(4), Traffic Class(8), Flow Label(20), Next Header(8), Payload Length(16), Hop Limit(8). Add Source(128) + Destination(128) = 320 bits = 40 bytes.

mnemonics.title: Extension Headers Order
mnemonics.content: HBoR DuAH ESP - Hop-by-Hop, Routing, Fragment, Destination Options, Authentication Header, ESP. Remember: HBoR DuAH ESP travels.

mnemonics.title: IPv6 Address Types
mnemonics.content: U.M.A - Unicast (One), Multicast (Many), Anycast (Nearest). No B for Broadcast.

mnemonics.title: IPv6 Address Prefixes
mnemonics.content: FE80 = Link Local, FC/FD = Unique Local, 2000 = Global Unicast. Remember: FE80 = First Mile (link), 2000 = To Go global.

acronyms.usage: Method for hosts to auto-configure IPv6 addresses without DHCPv6 server.
Maximum packet size a network can handle. IPv6 minimum is 1280 bytes.
ICMPv6 protocol for address resolution, router discovery, and neighbor unreachability detection.
Process to find minimum MTU along path to avoid fragmentation in IPv6.
Method to generate 64-bit interface identifier from 48-bit MAC address.
Error reporting and diagnostics for IPv6, also used in neighbor discovery.
IPv6 extension header providing data integrity and authentication.
IPv6 extension header providing confidentiality and optional authentication.

visualTips.visualization: Draw 8 rows of 4-byte boxes. Row 1: Version(4) + Traffic Class(8) + Flow Label(20). Row 2: Payload Length(16) + Next Header(8) + Hop Limit(8). Rows 3-4: Source Address (128 bits). Rows 5-6: Destination Address (128 bits). Total = 40 bytes fixed.
Imagine a train: Base Header (engine) + Extension Headers (cars) + Data (cargo). Each car has 'Next Header' pointing to next car. Last car points to TCP/UDP.
Think of :: as a magic eraser. It removes consecutive zero groups. Example: 2001:0db8:0000:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334 = 2001:db8::8a2e:370:7334. Only one :: allowed!
In IPv6, routers can't chew (fragment) - only source can. Like a delivery truck that must know the road's width (MTU) before leaving, or send multiple smaller trucks.

analogies.analogy: IPv4 has ~4 billion addresses like fitting everyone in one small town. IPv6 has 340 undecillion addresses like having an address for every atom on Earth.
IPv6 header is like a standardized shipping container - always 40 feet (bytes). IPv4 is like varying box sizes, making routing slower due to header processing.
It's like postal service refusing to repack packages - sender must send appropriately sized parcels. This simplifies router operations.
Flow Label is like priority lane on a highway - packets with same label get same QoS treatment, like express delivery trucks.
